hero.title: Start from a single spark of inspiration and let AI guide your first novel
hero.description: Follow this path and you won't need to write an outline, build a character sheet, or plan volumes up front. You just supply the idea and make the key calls — AI breaks the whole book into steps that can keep moving.

modelNotice.title: Configure a model before you start writing
modelNotice.description: Auto Director, drafting, and chapter review all need a working model. Set up the provider, API key, and default model first — the rest of the workflow will run far more smoothly.

guide.title: Recommended path
guide.description: On your first run, follow this path and you'll go from idea to writable chapter.

configureModel.title: Configure your model
configureModel.description: In system settings, set up the provider, API key, and default model first — Auto Director and chapter execution depend on it.

inputInspiration.title: Type a one-line spark
inputInspiration.description: A genre, a protagonist, a conflict, or even a fuzzy idea is enough — AI will shape it into a workable direction for the book.

letAutoDirector.title: Let Auto Director open the book
letAutoDirector.description: The system generates a whole-book direction, title set, and opening prep, breaking complex planning into steps you can confirm.

confirmDirection.title: Confirm the direction
confirmDirection.description: Pick the candidate you most want to write; if nothing fits, generate more or refine just the one you chose.

advanceToWriting.title: Advance to writable state
advanceToWriting.description: Let AI keep building the main storyline, characters, volume strategy, and chapter breakdown until the project is ready to draft.

enterChapterExecution.title: Enter chapter execution
enterChapterExecution.description: Write, review, and fix chapter by chapter; when you want to move faster, let the whole-book pipeline keep pushing forward.

reviewTasks.title: Track tasks and follow-ups
reviewTasks.description: Task Center handles failed, queued, and running jobs; the Director follow-up page consolidates quality issues that need cleanup.

goals.title: Pick an entry point by goal
goals.description: You don't need to understand every feature — pick the entry that matches what you want to get done.

startFromScratch.title: Start from scratch
startFromScratch.description: For when you only have an idea — no clear genre, hook, protagonist, or opening arc yet.

continueProject.title: Continue a project
continueProject.description: Jump back to your novel list, pick the book most worth continuing, then finish the plan or write the next chapter.

configureProvider.title: Configure model provider
configureProvider.description: Fill in the provider endpoint, API key, and default model so the writing pipeline always has a model to call.

handleTasks.title: Handle tasks
handleTasks.description: Review running, queued, and failed background tasks — prioritize anything blocking your next chapter.

directorFollowUps.title: Director follow-ups
directorFollowUps.description: Work through the quality items Auto Director set aside so the whole book can keep moving toward done.

tuneStyle.title: Tune the writing style
tuneStyle.description: Distill the prose you like into a reusable style asset, then bind it to a novel to keep that voice consistent.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: When something feels unclear, start here.

needOutline.question: Do I need to know how to outline?
needOutline.answer: No. On your first run, just type a one-line spark and let Auto Director propose a whole-book direction — then confirm step by step.

knowledgeBaseRequired.question: Is the knowledge base required?
knowledgeBaseRequired.answer: Not required. Use Auto Director first to push the book to a writable state; reach for the knowledge base when you need references, book analyses, or long-term lore.

taskFailed.question: What if a task fails?
taskFailed.answer: Open Task Center to see why it failed. Once you've handled the usual model, network, or auth issues, return to the project and continue.

qualityPending.question: What does "quality pending" mean?
qualityPending.answer: It means the system logged a minor quality issue but kept writing the whole book first, so those chapters can be polished together later.
